---
id: "19.2.8"
title: "HCI Cluster Balance and Per-Node Skew Detection"
criticality: "high"
splunkPillar: "Observability"
---

# UC-19.2.8 · HCI Cluster Balance and Per-Node Skew Detection

## Description

Measures vendor-agnostic per-node imbalance in hyperconverged clusters by normalizing vSAN, Nutanix, HyperFlex, and Azure Stack HCI feeds into one schema, then quantifies skew on storage utilization percent, IOPS, and virtual machine counts against each cluster mean with parallel standard deviation context for triage, distinct from pool-level capacity growth (UC-19.2.2), latency-only views (UC-19.2.3), configured failure domains (UC-19.2.16), AHV-only commit headroom (UC-19.2.19), and vSAN cluster slack roll-ups (UC-19.2.27).

## Value

Hot nodes hide in cluster-wide averages: evacuations take longer, migrations contend, and patch waves hit the same hardware while siblings idle. A single multisearch plus governance thresholds gives owners a prioritized row that explains whether storage, I/O fan-out, or VM density is driving the imbalance so Distributed Resource Scheduler tuning, affinity audits, and planned hardware adds happen before an outage forces a messy drain.

## Implementation

Deploy the four ingestion paths or a subset, publish hci_cluster_balance_owner.csv, install the comment macro if missing, paste the multisearch SPL into a thirty-minute saved search, map severities to queues, and add dashboards for balance score and per-node deviation bars.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites

This use case is the per-node distribution-skew axis for hyperconverged estates. It is intentionally not the shared storage pool growth story in UC-19.2.2, not the configured failure-domain and rack-awareness posture in UC-19.2.16, not the AHV-only headroom and guest commit math in UC-19.2.19, and not the vSAN cluster-wide slack reserve headline in UC-19.2.27. The customer outcome is an early warning when one node carries a disproportionate share of stored utilization, I/O operations, or running virtual machines compared with siblings in the same cluster, which often precedes painful evacuations, uneven patch waves, and locality regressions even while aggregate free space still looks adequate on another dashboard.

Multisearch rationale: Splunk receives four different vendor schemas with different JSON keys and polling cadence. A single search that ORs every sourcetype without normalizing fields tends to produce null arithmetic and silent misses when a key moves between snake_case and camelCase after a firmware or Technical Add-on upgrade. Four explicit subsearches keep each arm readable, let you tune a heavy REST poller on one stack without rewriting the other three, and keep the outer statistical math identical across vendors.

Exact primary sourcetypes per vendor as cited in dataSources and Step 1: vmware:perf at the host interval for cpu.usage.average, mem.usage.average, and disk.maxTotalLatency.latest style counters when your forwarding mapping emits them; vmware:vsan:host_runtime for PowerCLI or REST flattened Get-VsanRuntimeInfo style bytes per node; vmware:vsphere:host for PowerCLI export of Get-VMHost inventory fields including cluster membership, NumCpu, MemoryTotalGB, and host identity; nutanix:host from Splunk_TA_nutanix; nutanix:host:throughput for custom Prism Element per-host stats including flattened controller IOPS; hyperflex:node:stats for HX Connect node statistics whether from Splunk Add-on for Cisco HyperFlex or a custom HEC poller; azure_stack_hci:node:stats and azure_stack_hci:vd:placement for scheduled PowerShell exports describing per-node storage jobs and virtual disk placement. Tertiary drift layers include vmware:vc:event for Distributed Resource Scheduler migrations, Nutanix acli vm.list_migrations style side streams when approved, and ucsm:event for Cisco UCS Manager maintenance context on UCS-backed HyperFlex footprints.

Indexes: standardize on index hci for merged multivendor payloads, or keep index vmware, nutanix, cisco, microsoft splits and OR them inside each multisearch arm exactly as deployed. HEC token policy: separate tokens per collector team if your security group requires blast-radius separation; rotate quarterly; enable acknowledgement on forwarders that implement retry. The governance lookup hci_cluster_balance_owner is published from lookups/hci_cluster_balance_owner.csv with transforms.conf stanza hci_cluster_balance_owner, min_matches zero, case sensitivity matching your naming standard. Column list for hci_cluster_balance_owner.csv: cluster_id, vendor, owner_team, target_max_skew_pct, target_max_iops_skew_pct, target_max_vmcount_skew_pct, change_freeze_until. Optional business_service or site_id columns may be added locally if joined in extended implementations.

Multisearch design rationale continued: separating arms avoids accidental field collision when two vendors reuse generic names like host_name for different entities, and it makes appInspect and code review easier because each stanza fits on one screen. The outer pipeline then treats vendor as a first-class column for lookup joins and for trellis dashboards.

Step 1 — Configure data collection

Per-vendor collection stanzas belong in a wrapper app such as TA_hci_cluster_balance. props.conf must set SHOULD_LINEMERGE=false, raise TRUNCATE for large JSON, and use INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS=json on indexers that parse JSON from HTTP Event Collector. Time zones must align to UTC in the poll_utc style field when you add one so skew math is not warped by local offsets on multinational estates.

VMware and vSAN: deploy Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder where your architecture standard requires it, then add the PowerCLI scheduled task on a Windows automation host that logs into vCenter with a least-privilege service account, emits one JSON line per host to vmware:vsphere:host with cluster_id and node_id keys you document, and emits vSAN runtime lines to vmware:vsan:host_runtime including capacity_max_gb and used_gb in gibibytes with one documented divisor. Map vmware:perf using metric name fields consistent with your inventory naming so the coalesce chain in the Splunk search can extract cpu and memory percentages from either pre-aggregated exports or live perf metrics.

Nutanix: install Splunk_TA_nutanix on a collector with outbound TCP 9440 to Prism Element. Enable nutanix:host inputs. Add the custom poller the Prism stats endpoint for each host UUID and post nutanix:host:throughput or enrich nutanix:host with controller_iops using coalesce-friendly field names at ingest. Document the metric list hypervisor_cpu_usage_ppm, hypervisor_memory_usage_ppm, and IOPS in the collector readme.

HyperFlex: prefer Splunk Add-on for Cisco HyperFlex when available in your entitlement path. Otherwise schedule a read-only HX Connect REST client mapping node_iops, node_throughput_mbps, cpu_pct_used, mem_pct_used, vm_count, and any capacity fields to hyperflex:node:stats. Pin API version in git because field renames happen between maintenance releases.

Azure Stack HCI: run scheduled PowerShell on a secure cluster jump host exporting per-node storage and placement to azure_stack_hci:node:stats and azure_stack_hci:vd:placement using ConvertTo-Json depth you tested with Splunk line breaking. Forward with HEC TLS and a token limited to the hci index.

Vendor-by-vendor mapping table for the common schema (print and pin in the ops wiki):

vsan maps cluster_id from vCenter cluster display name; node_id from host short name; cpu_pct_used and mem_pct_used from perf or export; iops from your chosen disk counter or interim zero if you only trust utilization skew initially; vm_count from host summary; capacity_max_gb and used_gb from vsan runtime export.

nutanix maps cluster_id from cluster_name; node_id from host_uuid or name; cpu and memory from ppm divided by ten thousand; iops from throughput stream; vm_count from num_vms when present; capacity_max_gb and used_gb from byte counters converted to gibibytes when the feed includes them.

hyperflex maps cluster_id from HX cluster identifier; node_id from node serial or management name per your standard; cpu_pct_used, mem_pct_used, iops, vm_count from REST; capacity_max_gb and used_gb when exposed else join rated capacity from CMDB in an extended search you govern elsewhere.

azure_stack_hci maps cluster_id from failover cluster name; node_id from cluster node name; cpu and memory from performance counters you publish; iops from storage counters; vm_count from inventory; capacity_max_gb and used_gb from pooled storage math you document because some feeds only provide cluster-level totals.

inputs.conf examples belong in your collector app: HTTP stanza for HEC, or a modular input calling a Python poller with interval 300 seconds for five-minute samples. Match interval to streamstats window guidance in Step 2.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Run the SPL below in Search, confirm one row per node, then save as alert hci_uc_19_2_8_cluster_skew with cron schedule */30 * * * * and time window latest @h. Route critical to on-call, high to infrastructure queue, warning to email and chat, info to dashboard-only consumption unless you are auditing placement trends in a cold archive.

Alert suppression: throttle on cluster_id and vendor for forty-five minutes when severity stays at warning while storage resync is active, but allow escalation if severity increases in the same window. Dispatch earliest -25h@h to feed the twenty-four hour streamstats smoothing; for quick interactive drills use a shorter window knowing balance_score_24h will be approximate.

Save-as-alert guidance: use numeric threshold fields from the lookup so governance can change targets without editing SPL. Keep uppercase AND and OR in combined predicates for readability and static review.

Severity routing narrative: critical reflects simultaneous breaches on storage skew, IOPS skew, and VM count skew versus lookup targets; high reflects two axes; warning reflects one axis; info is baseline or freeze-demoted. Demotion during change_freeze_until preserves visibility in the table while muting pages.

Fenced SPL for runbooks (must match the spl JSON field in production aside from index names you pre-approved):

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.8 HCI cluster per-node distribution skew across vSAN, Nutanix, HyperFlex, Azure Stack HCI. Tunables: hci_cluster_balance_owner.csv (target_max_skew_pct, target_max_iops_skew_pct, target_max_vmcount_skew_pct). Owner: owner_team. Freeze: change_freeze_until demotes severity to info when active. Multisearch merges four vendor arms into cluster_id, node_id, vendor, cpu_pct_used, mem_pct_used, iops, vm_count, capacity_max_gb, used_gb. Tune streamstats window=288 for 5m polls or 48 for 30m. Each arm: earliest=-25h@h latest=@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=hci OR index=vmware) (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:host_runtime" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:host" OR sourcetype="vmware:perf") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_id, host_name, hostName, name, hostname, host, entity, HypervisorHostname))
      | eval mkey=lower(toString(coalesce(metric, metric_name, MetricName, "")))
      | eval vnum=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, val, latest, "0")), 10)
      | eval cpu_metric=if(match(mkey, "cpu\.usage") OR match(mkey, "cpu.usage"), vnum, null())
      | eval mem_metric=if(match(mkey, "mem\.usage") OR match(mkey, "mem.usage") OR match(mkey, "memory\.usage"), vnum, null())
      | eval cpu_pct_used=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cpu_pct_used, cpu_usage_average, CpuUsage, cpu_metric, "0")), 10)
      | eval mem_pct_used=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(mem_pct_used, mem_usage_average, MemUsage, mem_metric, "0")), 10)
      | eval iops=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(iops, host_iops, disk_iops, est_iops, "0")), 10)
      | eval vm_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(vm_count, vmCount, num_vms, numVMs, "0")), 10)
      | eval capacity_max_gb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(capacity_max_gb, vsan_disk_capacity_gb, disk_capacity_gb, MemoryTotalGB, memory_total_gb, mem_total_gb, "0")), 10)
      | eval used_gb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(used_gb, vsan_used_gb, logical_used_gb, vsan_logical_used_gb, storage_used_gb, "0")), 10)
      | eval vendor="vsan"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(cpu_pct_used) AS cpu_pct_used latest(mem_pct_used) AS mem_pct_used latest(iops) AS iops latest(vm_count) AS vm_count latest(capacity_max_gb) AS capacity_max_gb latest(used_gb) AS used_gb BY cluster_id node_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) (sourcetype="nutanix:host" OR sourcetype="nutanix:host:throughput") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_id, cluster_uuid, clusterUuid))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(host_uuid, uuid, hostUuid, name, host_name))
      | eval hcpu=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(hypervisor_cpu_usage_ppm, "0")), 10)
      | eval hmem=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(hypervisor_memory_usage_ppm, "0")), 10)
      | eval cpu_pct_used=if((hcpu>0), round((hcpu/10000.0), 2), tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cpu_pct_used, "0")), 10))
      | eval mem_pct_used=if((hmem>0), round((hmem/10000.0), 2), tonumber(tostring(coalesce(mem_pct_used, "0")), 10))
      | eval iops=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(iops, controller_iops, read_iops, write_iops, "0")), 10)
      | eval vm_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(num_vms, numVms, vm_count, "0")), 10)
      | eval capb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(storage_capacity_bytes, disk_capacity_bytes, capacity_bytes, "0")), 10)
      | eval usedb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(used_storage_bytes, used_bytes, "0")), 10)
      | eval capacity_max_gb=if((capb>1000000), round((capb/1073741824.0), 2), tonumber(tostring(coalesce(capacity_max_gb, "0")), 10))
      | eval used_gb=if((usedb>1000000), round((usedb/1073741824.0), 2), tonumber(tostring(coalesce(used_gb, "0")), 10))
      | eval vendor="nutanix"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(cpu_pct_used) AS cpu_pct_used latest(mem_pct_used) AS mem_pct_used latest(iops) AS iops latest(vm_count) AS vm_count latest(capacity_max_gb) AS capacity_max_gb latest(used_gb) AS used_gb BY cluster_id node_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=cisco) sourcetype="hyperflex:node:stats" earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster_name, clusterName, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_id, nodeId, node_name, host_name, serialNumber, serial))
      | eval cpu_pct_used=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cpu_pct_used, cpuPctUsed, "0")), 10)
      | eval mem_pct_used=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(mem_pct_used, memPctUsed, "0")), 10)
      | eval iops=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(node_iops, nodeIops, iops, "0")), 10)
      | eval vm_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(vm_count, vmCount, "0")), 10)
      | eval capacity_max_gb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(storage_capacity_gb, capacity_gb, "0")), 10)
      | eval used_gb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(used_storage_gb, storage_used_gb, "0")), 10)
      | eval vendor="hyperflex"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(cpu_pct_used) AS cpu_pct_used latest(mem_pct_used) AS mem_pct_used latest(iops) AS iops latest(vm_count) AS vm_count latest(capacity_max_gb) AS capacity_max_gb latest(used_gb) AS used_gb BY cluster_id node_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=microsoft) (sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:node:stats" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:vd:placement") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_id, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_name, NodeName, name, fqdn, "UNKNOWN_NODE"))
      | eval cpu_pct_used=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cpu_pct_used, cpu_percent, "0")), 10)
      | eval mem_pct_used=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(mem_pct_used, mem_percent, "0")), 10)
      | eval iops=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(node_iops, storage_iops, "0")), 10)
      | eval vm_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(vm_count, vm_on_node, "0")), 10)
      | eval capacity_max_gb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(storage_pool_capacity_gb, capacity_gb, "0")), 10)
      | eval used_gb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(storage_used_gb, used_gb_export, "0")), 10)
      | eval vendor="azure_stack_hci"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(cpu_pct_used) AS cpu_pct_used latest(mem_pct_used) AS mem_pct_used latest(iops) AS iops latest(vm_count) AS vm_count latest(capacity_max_gb) AS capacity_max_gb latest(used_gb) AS used_gb BY cluster_id node_id vendor ]
| where (isnotnull(cluster_id)) AND (isnotnull(node_id)) AND (cluster_id!="UNKNOWN_CLUSTER") AND (len(toString(node_id))>0)
| fillnull value=0 iops vm_count cpu_pct_used mem_pct_used
| eval storage_util_pct=if((capacity_max_gb>0), round(((100.0*used_gb)/capacity_max_gb), 2), null())
| eventstats avg(cpu_pct_used) AS cluster_mean_cpu stdev(cpu_pct_used) AS cluster_stdev_cpu BY cluster_id
| eventstats avg(storage_util_pct) AS cluster_mean_storage stdev(storage_util_pct) AS cluster_stdev_storage BY cluster_id
| eventstats avg(iops) AS cluster_mean_iops stdev(iops) AS cluster_stdev_iops BY cluster_id
| eventstats avg(vm_count) AS cluster_mean_vms stdev(vm_count) AS cluster_stdev_vms BY cluster_id
| eval cpu_skew_pct=if((cluster_mean_cpu>0.01), round(((abs(cpu_pct_used - cluster_mean_cpu))/cluster_mean_cpu*100.0), 2), 0)
| eval storage_skew_pct=if((isnotnull(cluster_mean_storage)) AND (cluster_mean_storage>0.01), round(((abs(storage_util_pct - cluster_mean_storage))/cluster_mean_storage*100.0), 2), 0)
| eval iops_skew_pct=if((cluster_mean_iops>0.01), round(abs(iops - cluster_mean_iops)/cluster_mean_iops*100.0, 2), 0)
| eval vm_count_skew_pct=if((cluster_mean_vms>0.01), round(abs(vm_count - cluster_mean_vms)/cluster_mean_vms*100.0, 2), 0)
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id vendor
    [| inputlookup hci_cluster_balance_owner
     | eval cluster_id=toString(cluster_id)
     | eval vendor=toString(vendor)
     | eval owner_team=toString(owner_team)
     | eval target_max_skew_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_skew_pct, "25")), 10)
     | eval target_max_iops_skew_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_iops_skew_pct, "35")), 10)
     | eval target_max_vmcount_skew_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_vmcount_skew_pct, "30")), 10)
     | eval change_freeze_until=toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))
     | fields cluster_id vendor owner_team target_max_skew_pct target_max_iops_skew_pct target_max_vmcount_skew_pct change_freeze_until ]
| eval ts=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_skew_pct, "25")), 10)
| eval ti=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_iops_skew_pct, "35")), 10)
| eval tv=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_vmcount_skew_pct, "30")), 10)
| eval hit_s=if((storage_skew_pct>=ts), 1, 0)
| eval hit_i=if((iops_skew_pct>=ti), 1, 0)
| eval hit_v=if((vm_count_skew_pct>=tv), 1, 0)
| eval hit_sum=hit_s + hit_i + hit_v
| eval sev0=case((hit_sum>=3), "critical", (hit_sum=2), "high", (hit_sum=1), "warning", 1=1, "info")
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((len(toString(change_freeze_until))>4) AND (isnotnull(ft)) AND (now()<ft), 1, 0)
| eval severity=if((freeze_active=1), "info", sev0)
| sort 0 + _time cluster_id node_id
| eval combined_skew=max(storage_skew_pct, max(iops_skew_pct, vm_count_skew_pct))
| streamstats window=288 current=t avg(combined_skew) AS rolling_combined_skew_mean BY cluster_id
| eval balance_score_24h=round(max(0, (100 - coalesce(rolling_combined_skew_mean, 0))), 2)
| table vendor cluster_id node_id storage_skew_pct iops_skew_pct vm_count_skew_pct cpu_skew_pct balance_score_24h severity owner_team change_freeze_until freeze_active cluster_mean_iops cluster_stdev_iops cluster_mean_vms cluster_stdev_vms
```

Step 3 — Validate

Cross-check VMware: compare each host tile in the vCenter cluster view to cpu_pct_used and mem_pct_used for the same node_id, then compare storage distribution ideas to storage_util_pct, recognizing UC-19.2.27 is where cluster slack is primary. Validate Nutanix: open Prism Cluster performance by host and compare relative host curves to Splunk skew fields for the same fifteen-minute window. HyperFlex: compare HX Connect cluster activity to the Splunk row for node_iops-shaped signals. Azure Stack HCI: Windows Admin Center cluster performance should show the same host ordering pattern as vm_count and CPU fields in Splunk during steady state.

Inject synthetic skew in lab by pinning about twenty small test VMs to one node with an affinity override or by temporarily blocking voluntary migrations, then confirm vm_count_skew_pct rises within two polling cycles and returns after removal. For storage-heavy tests, clone thin disks to one node when safe in non-production. Compare optional vmware:vc:event during the test to see migration attempts blocked by policy, which explains legitimate skew that is not a random hot spot.

Maintenance-mode false-positive guard: when a host is in draining maintenance, low utilization on that node paired with elevated siblings is expected and should be read beside maintenance timestamps and optional event overlays rather than as a mystery imbalance.

Step 4 — Operationalize

Dashboards: cluster balance score as a single value using mean of balance_score_24h by cluster_id; per-node deviation bar chart sorting storage_skew_pct or vm_count_skew_pct; skew trend timechart using rolling_combined_skew_mean from a summarized copy if interactive performance demands it; a heat of vendor by severity for executive review.

Runbook: rebalance trigger when critical fires and placement is unintentional; Distributed Resource Scheduler or Acropolis Dynamic Scheduling rule audit when skew persists through two consecutive alert intervals; capacity-versus-compute imbalance ticket type when skew arises from asymmetric hardware generations and cannot be fixed by moves alone. Ownership stays with platform engineering for SPL and lookup rows; application owners document deliberate imbalance in CMDB-linked notes.

Tie-in references: use UC-19.2.16 to avoid solving skew by concentrating VMs into one fault domain; use UC-19.2.19 when Nutanix AHV host-capacity commit must be read next to this skew signal; use UC-19.2.27 when vSAN slack is the gating risk for evacuating a hot node.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

Case one: vendor field renames after a firmware upgrade yield null cpu_pct_used. Mitigation: extend coalesce lists in each multisearch arm, capture fieldsummary output to git, and add FIELDALIAS in your app rather than disabling the alert.

Case two: mixed-generation nodes with different raw DIMM and disk capacities make absolute used_gb look unfair. Mitigation: keep storage skew on storage_util_pct using per-node capacity_max_gb, and optionally normalize IOPS by CMDB rated IOPS in a localized extension.

Case three: missing sourcetype after a Technical Add-on upgrade means a modular input disabled or renamed. Mitigation: diff default inputs.conf between versions on the collector and restore forwarding paths with an explicit sourcetype override until you finish migration.

Case four: explicit affinity for licensed software clusters creates sustained vm_count skew. Mitigation: annotate hci_cluster_balance_owner with higher tolerances for that cluster_id or maintain a supplemental suppression macro governed by change control.

Case five: maintenance mode shows fake under-utilization on the drained node. Mitigation: set change_freeze_until through maintenance, enrich with maintenance events, or filter known drained hosts via a small lookup of node_id you refresh nightly.

Case six: post-disk-replacement one-shot rebuild inflates iops_skew_pct briefly. Mitigation: require two consecutive elevated samples before paging or add a short rolling median on iops in a child summary used only for paging, not for forensic tables.

Case seven: multi-rack stretched deployments must not compute one cluster_mean across intentionally asymmetric sites during partial failover drills. Mitigation: split cluster_id per site in ingest or add site to the group-by fields in a forked SPL variant your team documents in the same repo.

Case eight: DRS-equivalent automation actively moving guests can create ten-minute spikes. Mitigation: rely on streamstats smoothing and optional event correlation from vmware:vc:event before calling a human at night.

Extended operational notes: keep lookup edits in version control with ticket numbers; run quarterly table-top reviews that walk one skew alert from Splunk row to vendor console with screenshots archived for auditors; when Kubernetes worker fleets on HCI use a different density model, exclude those node classes with a clear filter to avoid comparing bare-metal orchestration nodes to generic VM hosts in the same stats.

Closing checklist for implementers: confirm hci_cluster_balance_owner columns exist exactly as listed in Prerequisites; confirm each multisearch arm returns non-zero events in a seven-day backfill; confirm macro comment exists so dispatch logs show UC identity; confirm indexes appear in role srchIndexesAllowed for operators.

Supplement A — Standard deviation versus mean-absent edge cases: when a cluster has only one visible node in the window stdev is null; treat that as a coverage gap, not balance perfection. supplement B — Poll cadence mismatches: HyperFlex ten-minute polls and VMware five-minute polls can be compared on latest() snapshots within the same wall clock by using stats latest before eventstats in extended forks if you observe beat interference. supplement C — Ownership churn: when owner_team rotates monthly, keep the CSV authoritative and let ticketing rules read email from the same field. supplement D — Capacity planning reviews: export monthly skew peaks even when severity stays info so finance sees trending concentration before thresholds breach. supplement E — Security reviews: restrict lookup visibility because owner_team often maps to internal LDAP groups. supplement F — Stretch goals: add rack_id to events offline and compute means within rack before cluster if UC-19.2.16 demands rack diversity in the same quarter. supplement G — Documentation hygiene: store a link to this JSON use case in Confluence with the Splunk app version table. supplement H — Backfill discipline: cold buckets should include at least thirty days before trusting streamstats. supplement I — ServiceNow: map severity to priority with critical as P2 for platform class clusters and P3 for dev tiers via a second lookup if needed. supplement J — Training: walk new engineers through a deliberate skew drill in lab with written rollback. supplement K — Idempotency: pollers must not double-post the same node without changing _time or dedupe logic will hide real skew. supplement L — Naming: cluster_id must match CMDB keys used in UC-19.2.16 to avoid duplicate human conclusions from mismatched strings. supplement M — Patch windows: skew often rises during firmware rolling updates; freeze fields are mandatory there. supplement N — Observability pillar alignment: keep dashboards under the same app prefix as related HCI content for ACL simplicity. supplement O — Upgrade testing: validate Splunk major upgrades with a saved search acceleration report. supplement P — Locale: keep timestamps ISO-8601 UTC to avoid freeze parsing bugs. supplement Q — Field quotas: if extract exceeds index field limits, use INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS selectively. supplement R — Data protection: do not log VM display names if policy forbids guest identity in metrics. supplement S — Multi-tenant: separate indexes per tenant only if isolation policy demands; otherwise prefer hci with tenant tag. supplement T — vSAN object count: optional future extension adds component counts per node, out of scope here.



## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.8 HCI cluster per-node distribution skew across vSAN, Nutanix, HyperFlex, Azure Stack HCI. Tunables: hci_cluster_balance_owner.csv (target_max_skew_pct, target_max_iops_skew_pct, target_max_vmcount_skew_pct). Owner: owner_team. Freeze: change_freeze_until demotes severity to info when active. Multisearch merges four vendor arms into cluster_id, node_id, vendor, cpu_pct_used, mem_pct_used, iops, vm_count, capacity_max_gb, used_gb. Tune streamstats window=288 for 5m polls or 48 for 30m. Each arm: earliest=-25h@h latest=@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=hci OR index=vmware) (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:host_runtime" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:host" OR sourcetype="vmware:perf") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, cluster_name, clusterName, cluster, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_id, host_name, hostName, name, hostname, host, entity, HypervisorHostname))
      | eval mkey=lower(toString(coalesce(metric, metric_name, MetricName, "")))
      | eval vnum=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value, val, latest, "0")), 10)
      | eval cpu_metric=if(match(mkey, "cpu\.usage") OR match(mkey, "cpu.usage"), vnum, null())
      | eval mem_metric=if(match(mkey, "mem\.usage") OR match(mkey, "mem.usage") OR match(mkey, "memory\.usage"), vnum, null())
      | eval cpu_pct_used=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cpu_pct_used, cpu_usage_average, CpuUsage, cpu_metric, "0")), 10)
      | eval mem_pct_used=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(mem_pct_used, mem_usage_average, MemUsage, mem_metric, "0")), 10)
      | eval iops=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(iops, host_iops, disk_iops, est_iops, "0")), 10)
      | eval vm_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(vm_count, vmCount, num_vms, numVMs, "0")), 10)
      | eval capacity_max_gb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(capacity_max_gb, vsan_disk_capacity_gb, disk_capacity_gb, MemoryTotalGB, memory_total_gb, mem_total_gb, "0")), 10)
      | eval used_gb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(used_gb, vsan_used_gb, logical_used_gb, vsan_logical_used_gb, storage_used_gb, "0")), 10)
      | eval vendor="vsan"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(cpu_pct_used) AS cpu_pct_used latest(mem_pct_used) AS mem_pct_used latest(iops) AS iops latest(vm_count) AS vm_count latest(capacity_max_gb) AS capacity_max_gb latest(used_gb) AS used_gb BY cluster_id node_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) (sourcetype="nutanix:host" OR sourcetype="nutanix:host:throughput") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_id, cluster_uuid, clusterUuid))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(host_uuid, uuid, hostUuid, name, host_name))
      | eval hcpu=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(hypervisor_cpu_usage_ppm, "0")), 10)
      | eval hmem=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(hypervisor_memory_usage_ppm, "0")), 10)
      | eval cpu_pct_used=if((hcpu>0), round((hcpu/10000.0), 2), tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cpu_pct_used, "0")), 10))
      | eval mem_pct_used=if((hmem>0), round((hmem/10000.0), 2), tonumber(tostring(coalesce(mem_pct_used, "0")), 10))
      | eval iops=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(iops, controller_iops, read_iops, write_iops, "0")), 10)
      | eval vm_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(num_vms, numVms, vm_count, "0")), 10)
      | eval capb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(storage_capacity_bytes, disk_capacity_bytes, capacity_bytes, "0")), 10)
      | eval usedb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(used_storage_bytes, used_bytes, "0")), 10)
      | eval capacity_max_gb=if((capb>1000000), round((capb/1073741824.0), 2), tonumber(tostring(coalesce(capacity_max_gb, "0")), 10))
      | eval used_gb=if((usedb>1000000), round((usedb/1073741824.0), 2), tonumber(tostring(coalesce(used_gb, "0")), 10))
      | eval vendor="nutanix"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(cpu_pct_used) AS cpu_pct_used latest(mem_pct_used) AS mem_pct_used latest(iops) AS iops latest(vm_count) AS vm_count latest(capacity_max_gb) AS capacity_max_gb latest(used_gb) AS used_gb BY cluster_id node_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=cisco) sourcetype="hyperflex:node:stats" earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster_name, clusterName, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_id, nodeId, node_name, host_name, serialNumber, serial))
      | eval cpu_pct_used=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cpu_pct_used, cpuPctUsed, "0")), 10)
      | eval mem_pct_used=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(mem_pct_used, memPctUsed, "0")), 10)
      | eval iops=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(node_iops, nodeIops, iops, "0")), 10)
      | eval vm_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(vm_count, vmCount, "0")), 10)
      | eval capacity_max_gb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(storage_capacity_gb, capacity_gb, "0")), 10)
      | eval used_gb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(used_storage_gb, storage_used_gb, "0")), 10)
      | eval vendor="hyperflex"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(cpu_pct_used) AS cpu_pct_used latest(mem_pct_used) AS mem_pct_used latest(iops) AS iops latest(vm_count) AS vm_count latest(capacity_max_gb) AS capacity_max_gb latest(used_gb) AS used_gb BY cluster_id node_id vendor ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=microsoft) (sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:node:stats" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:vd:placement") earliest=-25h@h latest=@h
      | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, cluster_id, "UNKNOWN_CLUSTER"))
      | eval node_id=toString(coalesce(node_name, NodeName, name, fqdn, "UNKNOWN_NODE"))
      | eval cpu_pct_used=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(cpu_pct_used, cpu_percent, "0")), 10)
      | eval mem_pct_used=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(mem_pct_used, mem_percent, "0")), 10)
      | eval iops=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(node_iops, storage_iops, "0")), 10)
      | eval vm_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(vm_count, vm_on_node, "0")), 10)
      | eval capacity_max_gb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(storage_pool_capacity_gb, capacity_gb, "0")), 10)
      | eval used_gb=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(storage_used_gb, used_gb_export, "0")), 10)
      | eval vendor="azure_stack_hci"
      | stats latest(_time) AS _time latest(cpu_pct_used) AS cpu_pct_used latest(mem_pct_used) AS mem_pct_used latest(iops) AS iops latest(vm_count) AS vm_count latest(capacity_max_gb) AS capacity_max_gb latest(used_gb) AS used_gb BY cluster_id node_id vendor ]
| where (isnotnull(cluster_id)) AND (isnotnull(node_id)) AND (cluster_id!="UNKNOWN_CLUSTER") AND (len(toString(node_id))>0)
| fillnull value=0 iops vm_count cpu_pct_used mem_pct_used
| eval storage_util_pct=if((capacity_max_gb>0), round(((100.0*used_gb)/capacity_max_gb), 2), null())
| eventstats avg(cpu_pct_used) AS cluster_mean_cpu stdev(cpu_pct_used) AS cluster_stdev_cpu BY cluster_id
| eventstats avg(storage_util_pct) AS cluster_mean_storage stdev(storage_util_pct) AS cluster_stdev_storage BY cluster_id
| eventstats avg(iops) AS cluster_mean_iops stdev(iops) AS cluster_stdev_iops BY cluster_id
| eventstats avg(vm_count) AS cluster_mean_vms stdev(vm_count) AS cluster_stdev_vms BY cluster_id
| eval cpu_skew_pct=if((cluster_mean_cpu>0.01), round(((abs(cpu_pct_used - cluster_mean_cpu))/cluster_mean_cpu*100.0), 2), 0)
| eval storage_skew_pct=if((isnotnull(cluster_mean_storage)) AND (cluster_mean_storage>0.01), round(((abs(storage_util_pct - cluster_mean_storage))/cluster_mean_storage*100.0), 2), 0)
| eval iops_skew_pct=if((cluster_mean_iops>0.01), round(abs(iops - cluster_mean_iops)/cluster_mean_iops*100.0, 2), 0)
| eval vm_count_skew_pct=if((cluster_mean_vms>0.01), round(abs(vm_count - cluster_mean_vms)/cluster_mean_vms*100.0, 2), 0)
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id vendor
    [| inputlookup hci_cluster_balance_owner
     | eval cluster_id=toString(cluster_id)
     | eval vendor=toString(vendor)
     | eval owner_team=toString(owner_team)
     | eval target_max_skew_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_skew_pct, "25")), 10)
     | eval target_max_iops_skew_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_iops_skew_pct, "35")), 10)
     | eval target_max_vmcount_skew_pct=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_vmcount_skew_pct, "30")), 10)
     | eval change_freeze_until=toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))
     | fields cluster_id vendor owner_team target_max_skew_pct target_max_iops_skew_pct target_max_vmcount_skew_pct change_freeze_until ]
| eval ts=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_skew_pct, "25")), 10)
| eval ti=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_iops_skew_pct, "35")), 10)
| eval tv=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_vmcount_skew_pct, "30")), 10)
| eval hit_s=if((storage_skew_pct>=ts), 1, 0)
| eval hit_i=if((iops_skew_pct>=ti), 1, 0)
| eval hit_v=if((vm_count_skew_pct>=tv), 1, 0)
| eval hit_sum=hit_s + hit_i + hit_v
| eval sev0=case((hit_sum>=3), "critical", (hit_sum=2), "high", (hit_sum=1), "warning", 1=1, "info")
| eval ft=coalesce(strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((len(toString(change_freeze_until))>4) AND (isnotnull(ft)) AND (now()<ft), 1, 0)
| eval severity=if((freeze_active=1), "info", sev0)
| sort 0 + _time cluster_id node_id
| eval combined_skew=max(storage_skew_pct, max(iops_skew_pct, vm_count_skew_pct))
| streamstats window=288 current=t avg(combined_skew) AS rolling_combined_skew_mean BY cluster_id
| eval balance_score_24h=round(max(0, (100 - coalesce(rolling_combined_skew_mean, 0))), 2)
| table vendor cluster_id node_id storage_skew_pct iops_skew_pct vm_count_skew_pct cpu_skew_pct balance_score_24h severity owner_team change_freeze_until freeze_active cluster_mean_iops cluster_stdev_iops cluster_mean_vms cluster_stdev_vms
```

## Visualization

Single value balance_score_24h by cluster with trellis by vendor; bar chart of vm_count_skew_pct by node; timechart of rolling skew mean; detail table matching the alert projection; optional overlay panels for vmware:vc:event migration counts.

## References

- [Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder (Splunkbase 5380)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5380)
- [Splunk Add-on for Nutanix (Splunkbase 5076)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5076)
- [Splunk HEC documentation](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/Splunk/latest/Data/UsetheHEC)
- [Microsoft Learn — Azure Stack HCI overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure-stack/hci/overview)
